steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Access Point Boundary · Theory Access Point Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1150 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Access Point Boundary contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Build Access Point Boundary evidence · Theory Access Point Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-access-point-boundary-15fb72" that uses case 2918 to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record. Concept boundary: contrast one accepted path, one near miss, and the claim that still needs transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Access Point Boundary model · Theory Access Point Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: An access point creates a separate copy of the bucket data and policies. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
